lessonOutlines.title: OT role in functional assessment — occupational profile, ICF model, top-down vs bottom-up approach, function-first screening
Activities of Daily Living — bathing, dressing, toileting, feeding, transfers, mobility basics — assessment, assistance grading, safety
Instrumental Activities of Daily Living — meal preparation, medication management, finances, transportation, home management — assessment and goal-setting
Cognition and safety — attention, memory, executive function, safety awareness, task sequencing, impulsivity, judgment during ADL
Visual neglect and perceptual deficits — unilateral spatial neglect, visual field loss, figure-ground, depth perception, mealtime and dressing implications
Falls risk and environmental assessment — intrinsic vs extrinsic risk factors, bathroom hazards, threshold risks, lighting, grab bars, fall prevention
Adaptive equipment basics — reachers, dressing aids, bath seats, raised toilet seats, one-handed techniques, equipment trial and training
Energy conservation basics — pacing, activity scheduling, work simplification, COPD and cardiac ADL implications, fatigue management
Goal-writing basics — SMART goals, client-centered approach, measurable ADL/IADL outcomes, long-term vs short-term goal structure
Discharge planning considerations — functional safety threshold, support system assessment, home modification vs higher-level care, interdisciplinary communication
ADL vs IADL recognition, safety risk recognition, adaptive equipment matching, cognitive barrier identification, discharge planning decisions, functional deficit interpretation

worksheetPlaceholders.title: IV Therapy + Infusion Safety student worksheet
IV Therapy + Infusion Safety answer key

lessonOutlines.title: Basic neuro assessment — LOC, orientation, pupil response, motor and sensory exam, speech
FAST/BE-FAST tool — facial droop, arm drift, speech, eyes, balance, time to call
GCS basics — eye, verbal, motor scoring and total score interpretation
Stroke recognition — FAST positive, sudden onset symptoms, cortical vs non-cortical signs
Stroke mimics — hypoglycemia, seizure post-ictal, Todd's paralysis, migraine, increased ICP signs
Altered LOC differential — AEIOU-TIPS, hypoglycemia, overdose, trauma, metabolic causes
OT functional implications — unilateral neglect, hemiplegia, aphasia, acute facial droop, ADL impact
Increased ICP recognition — Cushing's triad, decreasing GCS, severe headache, vomiting, unequal pupils
Altered LOC with low glucose — glucose check priority, dextrose administration, mimic recognition
When to escalate — stroke centre transport, altered LOC triggers, time-sensitive decisions, glucose requirement
FAST/BE-FAST recognition, GCS scoring, stroke vs mimic identification, altered LOC priority, escalation timing

rapidDrillSets.title: Pharmacology Pattern Recognition recognition drill

lessonOutlines.title: Movement assessment basics — joint mobility, active vs passive ROM, strength grading, functional movement screening, pain and instability differentiation
Range of motion basics — joint-specific normal ROM values, goniometry, end-feel classification, hypomobility vs hypermobility, functional ROM thresholds
Strength grading basics — MRC grading scale 0–5, gravity-eliminated positions, functional strength indicators, grading reliability
Gait observation basics — gait cycle phases, normal vs abnormal patterns, antalgic gait, Trendelenburg sign, steppage/foot drop gait, scissor gait
Pain vs weakness vs instability — clinical differentiation, provocation testing, joint stability screening, functional task correlation
Ankle sprain — inversion mechanism of injury, Ottawa ankle rules, lateral ligament complex, grading I–III, acute management and rehab progression phases
ACL injury — mechanism of injury valgus pivot contact and non-contact, Lachman test and anterior drawer test, effusion, instability, conservative vs surgical pathway
Rotator cuff injury — impingement vs tear, painful arc, supraspinatus, Neer sign and Hawkins-Kennedy test, strength deficits, functional limitation
Low back strain — mechanism, red flag screening including cauda equina, SLR test, postural assessment, acute vs chronic LBP management basics
Hip fracture recovery basics — ORIF and hemiarthroplasty, post-op precautions, weight-bearing status, transfer safety, functional mobility milestones
Red flags in PT assessment — cauda equina syndrome, spinal cord compromise, fracture suspicion, malignancy, vascular claudication, saddle anesthesia, bilateral LE weakness
Rehab progression — acute phase protection and RICE, mobility phase ROM restoration, strengthening phase progressive resistance, return to function sport and task-specific goals

injury pattern recognition from mechanism and physical findings, ROM limitation interpretation, gait abnormality recognition, safe rehab progression decisions, red flag identification and referral criteria
